title.zh: 为西王母取食传信的神鸟
title.en: Divine Birds that Serve the Queen Mother of the West

summary.zh: 栖居三危之山的三只青鸟，为西王母取食；汉代以后化为传信的使者，“青鸟”从此成为信使的代称。
summary.en: Three azure birds roosting on Mount Sanwei who fetch food for the Queen Mother of the West; from Han times they became her heralds, and 'azure bird' has meant 'messenger' in Chinese ever since.

description.zh:
《西次三经》记三危之山“三青鸟居之。是山也，广员百里”。郭璞注：这就是为西王母取食的三青鸟，平日另栖于此山。《海内北经》写它们当值的情形：“西王母梯几而戴胜杖，其南有三青鸟，为西王母取食。”《大荒西经》给出它们的名字与模样：赤首黑目，一名大鵹，一名少鵹，一名青鸟。

汉晋以降，青鸟从女神的膳夫变成了信使。《汉武故事》写七月七日，忽有青鸟从西方飞来集于殿前，东方朔说：“这是西王母要来了。”顷刻王母果然驾到，两只青鸟夹侍在旁。唐诗把这个典故用到极致，李商隐《无题》“蓬山此去无多路，青鸟殷勤为探看”之后，“青鸟”在汉语里就是传信人的代称。

它们栖居的三危山，传统比定为敦煌东南的三危山，山谷对面就是莫高窟；同山还住着食人的白牛獓狠。
description.en:
The 'Classic of the Western Mountains' notes of Mount Sanwei only that 'the three azure birds dwell there' on a mountain 'a hundred li around'; Guo Pu explains that these are the birds who fetch food for the Queen Mother of the West, roosting here when off duty. The 'Classic of Regions Within the Seas: North' shows them at work — 'south of the Queen Mother, three azure birds fetch her food' — and the 'Classic of the Great Wilderness: West' names them: Dali, Shaoli, and Qingniao, red-headed and black-eyed.

From Han times onward the birds turned from cooks into couriers. In the Han Wudi Gushi, on the seventh day of the seventh month an azure bird flies in from the west and settles before the palace hall; Dongfang Shuo announces that the Queen Mother is coming, and presently she arrives with two azure birds flanking her. Tang poets fixed the motif for good — after Li Shangyin's line 'azure bird, be diligent and spy out the way for me', the word qingniao has simply meant 'messenger' in literary Chinese.

Their mountain is traditionally identified with the Sanwei hills southeast of Dunhuang, across the valley from the Mogao caves; they share it with the man-eating white ox Aohen.

value.zh: 大鵹、少鵹、青鸟（《大荒西经》）
value.en: Dali, Shaoli, and Qingniao, per the Great Wilderness classic
Azure birds with red heads and black eyes
Fetching food and bearing messages for the Queen Mother

sources.en: Shanhaijing, 'Classic of Regions Within the Seas: North'
Shanhaijing, 'Classic of the Great Wilderness: West'
Han Wudi Gushi (Tales of Emperor Wu of Han)
